title: Reading Aperture Rail
prompt:
Create a production-ready Reading Aperture Rail using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original scroll effect places a short internal reading pane beside a narrow aperture rail. As the user scrolls the pane, the rail’s opening moves to the current section and a concise chapter label updates. Keep the whole pattern self-contained: white 22px panel on #F4F5F6, #ECECEE border, soft shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F body copy. Use emerald as the only bright accent. Do not use gradients, full-page scroll listeners, or fixed positioning.

Use a focusable article with a real onScroll handler and three meaningful sections. The rail is decorative and aria-hidden; a nearby labelled status must expose the current section and use aria-live polite. The article should accept wheel, touch, Page Down, and keyboard scrolling naturally. Calculate active section from the article’s own scroll position in local state. Move the aperture with a stiffness 300, damping 26 spring and fade the status copy over 160ms ease-out. Avoid timers, random values, storage, network requests, remote assets, and dynamic code.

At 320px, keep the rail and reading text legible without clipping; at desktop sizes, maintain a useful compact preview and allow natural expansion. Provide a strong visible focus ring, semantic headings, logical order, AA contrast, and no dependence on hover. Honor prefers-reduced-motion by immediately placing the aperture at the computed section. Return complete client code and a runnable CSS approximation with suggested HTML.
